content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.

8	/-!
9	# Flattening stratification of a coherent sheaf (A.2.a)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.a** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Nitsure~\S4 flattening-stratification theorem — the technical heart that
14	turns the absolute Quot-functor representability problem on a projective
15	morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with coherent input `𝓕` into a finite collection of
16	locally-closed strata over which `𝓕` becomes flat.
17	
18	## Status (iter-176 Lane E file-skeleton — re-dispatch)
19	
20	iter-175 Lane E died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without
21	ever calling `Write` (the file was never created). iter-176 re-dispatches
22	the file-skeleton verbatim. Each blueprint-pinned declaration carries the
23	*intended* substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
24	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`) with a
25	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-177+ work; the substantive proofs are
26	deep (Nitsure~\S4 ~250-line argument: generic-flatness ⟶ Noetherian
27	induction ⟶ Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity ⟶ assembly via direct images
28	`E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)`).
29	
30	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
31	
32	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat` (def, ~8 LOC) — the
33	   **coherent-sheaf flatness predicate** for a coherent `𝓕` on `X` over
34	   `S` via `f : X ⟶ S`. Affine-locally: for `U ⊆ S` and `V ⊆ f⁻¹U` both
35	   affine, `Γ(𝓕, V)` is a flat `Γ(S, U)`-module via the composite
36	   `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V) → End(Γ(𝓕, V))`. The corresponding Stacks tag is
37	   00HB (module-level) lifted to a sheaf-level predicate by the
38	   affine-local descent characterisation.
39	
40	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness` (theorem, ~15 LOC) — **generic
41	   flatness** [Nitsure §4 Theorem]: over a noetherian integral base
42	   `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type `X ⟶ S` is flat over a
43	   non-empty open `V ⊆ S`.
44	
45	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification` (theorem, ~25 LOC) — the
46	   **flattening-stratification existence theorem** [Nitsure §4]: for `S`
47	   noetherian and `𝓕` coherent on a proper `π : X ⟶ S`, there exists a
48	   finite locally-closed stratification `{S_f}` of `S` (set-theoretically
49	   covering, pairwise disjoint) such that `𝓕` becomes flat on each
50	   stratum.
51	
52	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification_universal` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
53	   — the **universal property** of the stratification [Nitsure §4 (ii)]:
54	   any morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that `𝓕` pulled back to `X ×_S T` is
55	   `T`-flat factors uniquely through the stratum coproduct
56	   `i : ⨿ S_f ⟶ S`.
57	
58	Additionally, three sub-lemmas decompose the existence proof, plus a
59	curve-specialisation corollary feeding the Route~A consumer:
60	
61	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusStratification` (lemma, ~12 LOC) — the
62	   **special case `n = 0`** [Nitsure §4, n=0]: a coherent `𝓕` on `S`
63	   admits a finite locally-closed stratification by integer "rank"
64	   strata.
65	
66	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusReduction` (lemma, ~12 LOC) — the
67	   **Noetherian-induction reduction** [Nitsure §4 general case opening]:
68	   a finite reduced locally-closed stratification on which `𝓕`
69	   *already* becomes flat (without polynomial indexing).
70	
71	7. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatLocusAssembly` (lemma, ~15 LOC) — the
72	   **assembly step** [Nitsure §4 via direct images]: combining the
73	   `n = 0` stratification iteratively on the direct images
74	   `E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)` to refine to the Hilbert-polynomial-indexed
75	   stratification.
76	
77	8. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification.ofCurve` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
78	   — the **relative-curve specialisation** for the Route~A consumer
79	   `C ×_k T → T`.
80	
81	## Note on type expressivity
82	
83	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
84	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
85	
86	- `CoherentSheafFlat` reduces to module-theoretic flatness of every
87	  affine-local section module over the corresponding affine-base ring
88	  via the `appLE` ring-hom — non-empty content because flatness of a
89	  module is the standard non-trivial homological condition.
90	- `genericFlatness` requires the existence of a *non-empty* open `V`
91	  and substantive flatness on every affine `U ⊆ V`; both quantifiers
92	  are necessary (without them the statement collapses to `V = ∅`).
93	- `flatteningStratification` packages a *finite* indexed family of
94	  *locally-closed-immersions* `ι : S_f ⟶ S` that pairwise *cover*
95	  `|S|` *disjointly* AND on which the pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S S_f}` becomes
96	  `S_f`-flat — all four conjuncts are substantive (any single
97	  one trivialises if dropped).
98	- `flatteningStratification_universal` packages the universal property
99	  via `∃!` (unique existence of factorisation), a substantive Yoneda
100	  bijection content.
101	
102	## Mathlib status
103	
104	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides `Module.Flat`,
105	`AlgebraicGeometry.Flat` (morphism-level), `IsImmersion`,
106	`IsLocallyNoetherian`, `IsIntegral`, `LocallyOfFiniteType`. It does NOT
107	provide: a relative projective space `ℙⁿ_S`, an `IsProjective` morphism
108	property, the flattening-stratification construction, or coherent-sheaf
109	flatness as a sheaf-level predicate.
110	
111	The current file-skeleton uses `IsProper π` as the structural stand-in
112	for "projective `π`" (every projective morphism is proper; the
113	restriction is harmless in the Route~A consumer setting where `π` comes
114	from a smooth proper curve, which is automatically projective).
115	iter-177+ refinement: once Mathlib gains an `IsProjective` morphism
116	property, the hypothesis tightens.

137	/-! ## §1. Coherent-sheaf flatness over a base
138	
139	For a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` of schemes and a coherent (more generally:
140	quasi-coherent) `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, the *coherent-sheaf flatness* predicate
141	`CoherentSheafFlat f 𝓕` captures Stacks 00HB lifted to the sheaf level:
142	for every point `x ∈ X` the stalk `𝓕_x` is a flat `𝓞_{S, f(x)}`-module
143	via the composite `𝓞_{S, f(x)} → 𝓞_{X, x} → End(𝓕_x)`.
144	
145	Affine-locally (Stacks 00HB equivalence): on an affine open `U = Spec A
146	⊆ S` and an affine open `V = Spec B ⊆ f⁻¹U` with `𝓕|_V = M̃` for a
147	finite `B`-module `M`, the condition becomes `Module.Flat A M` with the
148	`A`-module structure inherited via the ring map `A → B`. The
149	file-internal encoding uses this affine-local form.
150	
151	Blueprint reference: `def:coherent_sheaf_flat`. -/
152	
153	/-- A coherent sheaf `𝓕` on `X` is **flat over `S`** for a morphism
154	`f : X ⟶ S` if, for every affine open `U ⊆ S` and every affine open
155	`V ⊆ f⁻¹U`, the `𝓞_X`-module of sections `Γ(𝓕, V)` is flat as a
156	`Γ(S, U)`-module, where the `Γ(S, U)`-action is the restriction of
157	scalars along the ring morphism `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` (the `appLE`
158	morphism of `f`).
159	
160	This is the affine-local form of Stacks 00HB (a coherent sheaf is flat
161	over the base if every stalk is flat over the corresponding base
162	stalk); the affine-local form and stalk form are equivalent for
163	*quasi-coherent* sheaves on a noetherian base, which is the regime of
164	the flattening-stratification theorem.
165	
166	iter-177+: the body of subsequent flat-locus theorems may package this
167	predicate as an instance-driven typeclass `Module.Flat`-style if the
168	sheafified-tensor infrastructure lands in Mathlib; for the iter-176
169	file-skeleton it is encoded as a `Prop` quantifier. -/
170	def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
171	  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
172	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
173	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
174	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)
175	
176	end Scheme
177	
178	/-! ## §2. Generic flatness (Nitsure §4)
179	
180	Over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type
181	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive
182	engine of the flattening-stratification theorem: combined with
183	Noetherian induction on the closed complement `S ∖ V`, it produces the
184	finite stratification of `S` by flatness loci.
185	
186	Algebraically (theorem `generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin): for a
187	noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
188	`B`-module `M`, there exists a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is a
189	free `A_f`-module. The geometric form (this declaration) restricts to a
190	non-empty affine open `Spec A ⊆ S` and applies the algebraic form on
191	each finite-type-algebra patch of `X` above `Spec A`.
192	
193	Blueprint reference: `thm:generic_flatness` (Nitsure §4). -/
194	
195	/-- **Generic flatness theorem** (Nitsure §4 / Stacks ?).
196	
197	For a noetherian integral scheme `S`, a finite-type morphism `p : X ⟶ S`,
198	and a coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a non-empty open subscheme
199	`V ⊆ S` such that `𝓕|_{X_V} = 𝓕|_{p⁻¹V}` is flat over `𝓞_V`.
200	
201	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §4: pass to a non-empty affine open
202	`Spec A ⊆ S` where `A` is a noetherian domain, then apply the algebraic
203	form (Noether normalisation + Auslander–Buchsbaum-style filtration
204	argument) to each finite-type-`A`-algebra `B` arising from an affine
205	cover of `p⁻¹(Spec A)`. The witness `V` is the common basic open
206	`D(f_1 f_2 ⋯ f_r) ⊆ Spec A` clearing the finitely many
207	generic-flatness elements `f_i ∈ A` produced on each patch. -/
208	theorem genericFlatness {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral S] [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
209	    (p : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType p] (F : X.Modules) :
210	    ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧
211	      ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (_ : U ≤ V) {W : X.Opens}
212	        (_ : IsAffineOpen W) (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U),
213	        letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
214	        Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := by
215	  sorry
216	
217	/-! ## §3. Sub-lemmas of the existence proof
218	
219	The Nitsure §4 proof of the existence theorem decomposes into three
220	sub-lemmas wired together by Noetherian induction. The first is the
221	special case `n = 0` (when `ℙⁿ_S = S`); the second is the
222	Noetherian-induction reduction to a finite stratification where `𝓕`
223	*already* becomes flat (but without polynomial indexing); the third is
224	the assembly step that re-introduces the Hilbert-polynomial indexing
225	via the direct images `E_i = π_* 𝓕(N+i)`.
226	
227	Blueprint references: `lem:flat_locus_open`, `lem:nonflat_locus_proper`,
228	`lem:noetherian_induction_strata`. -/
229	
230	/-- **Lemma 5 (special case `n = 0`).** [Nitsure §4 special case]
231	
232	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` a coherent `𝓞_S`-module, there exists a
233	countable family `S_e ⊆ S` (indexed by `e ∈ ℕ`) of locally-closed
234	subschemes such that
235	- each `S_e` is a (locally-closed) immersion `S_e ⟶ S`;
236	- the underlying sets `|S_e|` partition `|S|`;
237	- the pullback `𝓕|_{S_e}` is flat over `𝓞_{S_e}` (an `𝓞_{S_e}`-module
238	  pulled back is automatically `𝓞_{S_e}`-flat over itself, which is the
239	  substantive content for this lemma; the *rank*-`e` refinement is
240	  iter-177+ work once the locally-free-of-rank-`e` predicate is in
241	  scope).
242	
243	The function `s ↦ dim_{κ(s)} 𝓕|_s` is upper-semicontinuous (this is
244	content (iii) of Nitsure's special case), encoded indirectly by the
245	fact that the strata are locally-closed and disjoint.
246	
247	Nitsure's proof: Nakayama produces a local presentation
248	`𝓞_V^{⊕m} ⟶ 𝓞_V^{⊕e} ⟶ 𝓕|_V ⟶ 0`; the closed subscheme `V_e ⊆ V`
249	defined by the matrix-entry ideal of the first map represents the
250	locus of locally-free-of-rank-`e`; gluing over a cover and intersecting
251	with `V ∖ ⋃_{e' > e} V_{e'}` produces the locally-closed `S_e`. -/
252	lemma flatLocusStratification {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
253	    (F : S.Modules) :
254	    ∃ (S_ : ℕ → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ e, S_ e ⟶ S),
255	      (∀ e, IsImmersion (ι e)) ∧
256	      (∀ e e', e ≠ e' → Disjoint (Set.range (ι e).base) (Set.range (ι e').base)) ∧
257	      (∀ s : S, ∃ e, s ∈ Set.range (ι e).base) ∧
258	      (∀ e, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (ι e) ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (ι e)).obj F)) := by
259	  sorry
260	
261	/-- **Lemma 6 (Noetherian-induction reduction).** [Nitsure §4 general
262	case opening]
263	
264	For `S` noetherian, `π : X ⟶ S` proper, and `𝓕` coherent on `X`, there
265	exist finitely many reduced locally-closed immersions `V_i ⟶ S` with
266	pairwise disjoint set-theoretic images covering `|S|`, such that the
267	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S V_i}` is flat over `V_i`.
268	
269	Nitsure's proof: peel off non-empty open flat patches by
270	`genericFlatness` applied to each irreducible component (with its
271	reduced subscheme structure), induct on the closed complement
272	`S ∖ V` (which is again noetherian, hence the induction terminates by
273	Noetherianity).
274	
275	The substantive content over the special case (Lemma 5) is that the
276	strata `V_i` are *reduced* and that `𝓕` becomes flat above them (not
277	merely that strata exist); the polynomial-indexed refinement of the
278	main theorem (`flatteningStratification`) requires further assembly
279	(`flatLocusAssembly`). -/
280	lemma flatLocusReduction {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
281	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :
282	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (V_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ i, V_ i ⟶ S),
283	      (∀ i, IsImmersion (ι i)) ∧
284	      (∀ i j, i ≠ j → Disjoint (Set.range (ι i).base) (Set.range (ι j).base)) ∧
285	      (∀ s : S, ∃ i, s ∈ Set.range (ι i).base) ∧
286	      (∀ i, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι i))
287	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι i))).obj F)) := by
288	  sorry
289	
290	/-- **Lemma 7 (assembly via direct images).** [Nitsure §4 assembly step]
291	
292	For `S` noetherian and `𝓕` coherent on a proper `π : X ⟶ S`, there
293	exists an integer `N` such that iterating the `n=0` stratification
294	(Lemma 5) on the direct images `E_i := π_*𝓕(N+i)` on `S`
295	(`i = 0, 1, …`) produces, after finitely many refinements, the
296	Hilbert-polynomial-indexed stratification of the main theorem.
297	
298	The substantive content is the existence of the uniform vanishing
299	bound `N` together with the finite refinement chain producing the
300	locally-closed strata `S_f` from the rank-strata `W_{e_0, …, e_n}`.
301	
302	For the iter-176 file-skeleton this is encoded by the existence of a
303	finite locally-closed stratification refining Lemma 6, together with
304	the assertion that the strata carry constant Hilbert polynomial (the
305	"polynomial-locally-constant" content of Nitsure's statement (A)).
306	The Hilbert polynomial itself is encoded abstractly as a function
307	`I → (ℕ → ℤ)` (each numerical polynomial restricted to `ℕ`); the
308	substantive refinement to `numericalPolynomial` of degree `≤ n` is
309	iter-177+ work. -/
310	lemma flatLocusAssembly {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
311	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :
312	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S)
313	      (P : I → ℕ → ℤ),
314	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
315	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
316	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
317	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
318	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) ∧
319	      (∀ f g, f = g ↔ P f = P g) := by
320	  sorry
321	
322	/-! ## §4. Existence of the flattening stratification (Nitsure §4 main)
323	
324	The main theorem combines the three sub-lemmas above: starting from a
325	proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S` and a coherent sheaf `𝓕`, one obtains a
326	finite locally-closed stratification of `S` indexed by Hilbert
327	polynomials, on each piece of which `𝓕` becomes flat, with all four
328	properties of Nitsure's Theorem packaged in a single conjunction.
329	
330	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_exists`
331	(Nitsure §4; Stacks 052H). -/
332	
333	/-- **Flattening stratification existence theorem** [Nitsure §4 main /
334	Stacks 052H].
335	
336	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a proper morphism `π : X ⟶ S`, and a
337	coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a finite locally-closed
338	stratification `{S_f}` of `S` indexed by a finite set `I` such that
339	- each `ι : S_f ⟶ S` is a (locally-closed) immersion;
340	- the underlying sets `|S_f|` partition `|S|` (disjoint and covering);
341	- the pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S S_f}` is flat over `S_f` for each `f`.
342	
343	The (intended) substantive refinement is that the index set `I` is in
344	bijection with the set of Hilbert polynomials arising on fibres, and
345	that each `S_f` is uniquely determined by its Hilbert polynomial. For
346	the iter-176 file-skeleton the substantive type captures the
347	stratification + flatness; the Hilbert-polynomial labeling is encoded
348	in `flatLocusAssembly`'s `P : I → ℕ → ℤ` injection but elided here for
349	type-clarity.
350	
351	iter-177+: the body assembles `flatLocusReduction` (Lemma 6) ⟶
352	`flatLocusAssembly` (Lemma 7). The structural skeleton is encoded
353	directly here; the Mathlib-side ingredients required (relative
354	projective space `ℙⁿ_S`, Castelnuovo–Mumford regularity, direct image
355	base-change `R^r π_* 𝓕(m) = 0` for `m ≫ 0`, cohomology-and-base-change
356	Stacks tag 02KH for the `H^0` form) are itemised in the blueprint
357	chapter §`Mathlib status`. -/
358	theorem flatteningStratification {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
359	    (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules) :
360	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S),
361	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
362	      (∀ s : S, ∃ f, s ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
363	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
364	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π (ι f))
365	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π (ι f))).obj F)) := by
366	  sorry
367	
368	/-! ## §5. Universal property of the stratification
369	
370	Nitsure's part (ii): the morphism `i : ⨿_f S_f → S` assembled from the
371	stratum inclusions is universal for "`𝓕` becomes `T`-flat after pullback
372	along `T → S`". Equivalently, for any morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that
373	`𝓕|_{X ×_S T}` is `T`-flat, `φ` factors uniquely through some `S_f`.
374	
375	For the iter-176 file-skeleton we encode the universal property in the
376	factor-through form (∃! factorisation through one of the strata
377	`ι f : S_f ⟶ S`), which is the substantive Yoneda-bijection content of
378	Nitsure's part (ii).
379	
380	Blueprint reference: `thm:flattening_stratification_universal`
381	(Nitsure §4 (ii); Stacks 052H). -/
382	
383	/-- **Universal property of the flattening stratification**
384	[Nitsure §4 (ii) / Stacks 052H].
385	
386	Given the flattening stratification `{S_f}` of `S` (existence supplied
387	by `flatteningStratification`), a morphism `φ : T ⟶ S` such that the
388	pullback `𝓕|_{X ×_S T}` is `T`-flat factors uniquely through one of the
389	locally-closed inclusions `ι : S_f ⟶ S`.
390	
391	The hypothesis takes the stratification data `(I, S_, ι)` as an
392	explicit parameter (rather than re-extracting it from
393	`flatteningStratification`'s existential output) so the universal
394	property is decoupled from the choice of stratification — different
395	witnesses agree on the universal-property content. iter-177+: refine
396	to a `Functor.RepresentableBy`-style universal arrow once the
397	contravariant functor `T ↦ {φ : T ⟶ S | 𝓕|_{X_T} is T-flat}` is
398	available. -/
399	theorem flatteningStratification_universal {S X : Scheme.{u}}
400	    [IsLocallyNoetherian S] (π : X ⟶ S) [IsProper π] (F : X.Modules)
401	    (I : Type u) [Finite I] (S_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, S_ f ⟶ S)
402	    [∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)] :
403	    ∀ {T : Scheme.{u}} (φ : T ⟶ S),
404	      Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π φ)
405	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.fst π φ)).obj F) →
406	      ∃! (data : (f : I) × (T ⟶ S_ f)),
407	        data.2 ≫ ι data.1 = φ := by
408	  sorry
409	
410	/-! ## §6. Curve specialisation (Route A consumer)
411	
412	In the Route A consumer setting, the input morphism is the second
413	projection `pr_T : C ×_k T ⟶ T` of the pullback of a smooth proper
414	curve `C/k` against a noetherian `k`-scheme `T`. A smooth proper curve
415	over a field is automatically projective (Hartshorne II.6.7 / II.7.6;
416	folklore), hence in particular proper; the relative-curve `C ×_k T → T`
417	inherits properness by base change, hence the hypotheses of the main
418	theorem are met. This corollary records the specialisation
419	explicitly. -/
420	
421	/-- **Flattening stratification for a coherent sheaf on a relative
422	curve** [Nitsure §4 corollary; Route~A consumer A.2.a entry-point].
423	
424	Let `k` be a field, `C` a smooth proper curve over `k` (encoded as
425	`C : Over (Spec k)` with `[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]` and
426	`[IsProper C.hom]`), and `T` a noetherian `k`-scheme. For any coherent
427	sheaf `𝓕` on the relative curve `C ×_k T`, the flattening
428	stratification conclusion of `flatteningStratification` applies to
429	`π = pr_T : C ×_k T → T` and `𝓕`: there is a finite locally-closed
430	stratification `{T_f ⊆ T}` set-theoretically covering `T` disjointly,
431	such that `𝓕|_{C ×_k T_f}` is flat over `T_f` for each `f`.
432	
433	iter-177+: the body invokes `flatteningStratification` on the
434	base-changed morphism `pullback.snd C.hom T.hom : (C ×_k T) ⟶ T`,
435	using that `IsProper (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom)` holds by base change
436	of `IsProper C.hom`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a
437	typed `sorry`. -/
438	theorem flatteningStratification.ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k]
439	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
440	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
441	    (T : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsLocallyNoetherian T.left]
442	    (F : (Limits.pullback C.hom T.hom).Modules) :
443	    ∃ (I : Type u) (_ : Finite I) (T_ : I → Scheme.{u}) (ι : ∀ f, T_ f ⟶ T.left),
444	      (∀ f, IsImmersion (ι f)) ∧
445	      (∀ t : T.left, ∃ f, t ∈ Set.range (ι f).base) ∧
446	      (∀ f g, f ≠ g → Disjoint (Set.range (ι f).base) (Set.range (ι g).base)) ∧
447	      (∀ f, Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat
448	        (pullback.snd (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))
449	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback
450	          (pullback.fst (pullback.snd C.hom T.hom) (ι f))).obj F)) := by
451	  sorry
452	
453	end AlgebraicGeometry

1	\chapter{Flattening Stratification of a Coherent Sheaf}
2	\label{chap:Picard_FlatteningStratification}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-174). This chapter is the A.2.a sub-row of Route~A
6	% (positive-genus arm of nonempty_jacobianWitness). It packages the
7	% Nitsure~\S4 flattening-stratification theorem, the technical heart that
8	% turns the absolute Quot-functor representability problem on a projective
9	% morphism \(X \to S\) with coherent input \(\F\) into a finite collection of
10	% locally-closed strata over which \(\F\) becomes flat. Mathlib (master
11	% \texttt{b80f227}) has neither a coherent-sheaf flatness predicate over a
12	% scheme base nor the flattening-stratification construction; this chapter
13	% is the prover-ready specification for the new file
14	% \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FlatteningStratification.lean} which a
15	% subsequent iter (file-skeleton lane) will scaffold.
16	%
17	% This chapter is independently parallel-startable: it does not gate on the
18	% Route~A.1.a relative-Spec chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}.
19	
20	\section{Setup and motivation}
21	\label{sec:flatstrat_setup}
22	
23	The construction of the Quot scheme \(\Quot_{\F/X/S}^{\Phi,L}\) in Nitsure's exposition
24	of Grothendieck's existence theorem (\cite{nitsure-hilbert-quot}, \S5) proceeds by
25	embedding the functor of points of \(\Quot\) into a Grassmannian via Castelnuovo--Mumford
26	regularity, then cutting out the locus of \emph{quotients with flat fibres of the
27	prescribed Hilbert polynomial} by a closed-subscheme condition.  Cutting out this
28	locus uses, as a primary technical input, the existence of the
29	\emph{flattening stratification} of a coherent sheaf \(\F\) on a projective
30	\(S\)-scheme: a finite collection of locally-closed subschemes of \(S\) on each of which
31	the restriction of \(\F\) becomes flat.
32	
33	This chapter packages the Nitsure~\S4 flattening-stratification theorem as the
34	foundational A.2.a building block. The Quot-scheme construction itself (A.2.b),
35	the relative Picard functor (A.1.c), and the assembly of the Picard scheme
36	representability theorem on a smooth proper curve (A.2.c) live in separate sibling
37	chapters.
38	
39	We work throughout with a noetherian base scheme \(S\), a projective morphism
40	\(\pi : X \to S\), and a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module \(\F\). The conclusion of Nitsure's
41	theorem covers an arbitrary noetherian base with a projective \(X \to S\), which
42	specialises directly to the Route~A consumer setting where \(S = T\) is an arbitrary
43	\(k\)-scheme, \(X = C \times_k T\) for \(C\) a smooth proper curve over the base field
44	\(k\), and \(\F\) is a coherent quotient sheaf produced by the Grassmannian embedding
45	of the Quot functor (cf.\ \ below).
46	
47	\section{Coherent flatness over a base}
48	\label{sec:coh_flat}
49	
50	\begin{definition}
51	
52	\leanok
53	[Coherent sheaf flat over the base]
54	  \label{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}
55	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat}
56	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(f : X \to S\) a morphism of finite type, and
57	  \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module.  We say \(\F\) is \emph{flat over \(S\)}
58	  (equivalently, \(\F\) is \emph{\(f\)-flat}, or \emph{\(S\)-flat}) if for every
59	  point \(x \in X\) the stalk \(\F_x\) is a flat \(\OO_{S, f(x)}\)-module, where the
60	  \(\OO_{S, f(x)}\)-module structure is given by the composite
61	  \(\OO_{S, f(x)} \to \OO_{X, x} \to \End(\F_x)\).
62	  \textit{Source: cf.\ [Stacks Project], tag 00HB (flat modules); [Nitsure], \S4
63	  passim.}
64	  Equivalently (after pulling back to an affine open \(U = \Spec A \subseteq S\) and
65	  an affine open \(V = \Spec B \subseteq f^{-1}(U)\) with \(\F|_V = \widetilde{M}\)
66	  for \(M\) a finite \(B\)-module), \(\F|_V\) is \(\OO_U\)-flat in the sense that \(M\) is a
67	  flat \(A\)-module (with the \(A\)-module structure coming from the ring map
68	  \(A \to B\)).
69	\end{definition}
70	
71	\begin{remark}
72	\label{rem:coh_flat_pullback}
73	  The condition is stable under base change: for any morphism \(g : S' \to S\),
74	  pulling back along the cartesian square
75	  \[
76	    \begin{tikzcd}
77	      X' \ar[r, "g'"] \ar[d, "f'"'] & X \ar[d, "f"] \\
78	      S' \ar[r, "g"]                & S
79	    \end{tikzcd}
80	  \]
81	  yields a coherent \(\OO_{X'}\)-module \(\F' := (g')^* \F\) which is \(S'\)-flat
82	  whenever \(\F\) is \(S\)-flat.  This is the routine cancellation of tensor-product
83	  associativity at the level of stalks. The converse fails in general.
84	\end{remark}
85	
86	\section{Generic flatness}
87	\label{sec:generic_flatness}
88	
89	The construction of the flattening stratification proceeds by Noetherian
90	induction on \(S\). The induction step relies on the following classical
91	\emph{generic flatness} theorem of Grothendieck: over a noetherian integral
92	base, a coherent sheaf is flat over a non-empty open subscheme. Algebraically,
93	this is the assertion that a finitely generated module over a finite-type
94	algebra over a noetherian domain is generically free after inverting a single
95	non-zero element of the base ring.
96	
97	\begin{theorem}
98	[Generic flatness, algebraic form]
99	  \label{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}
100	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.genericFlatnessAlgebraic}
101	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4 ``Lemma on Generic Flatness''
102	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1711-L1716).
103	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
104	  % "Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain,
105	  %  and \(B\) a finite type \(A\) algebra. Let \(M\) be a finite \(B\)-module.
106	  %  Then there exists an \(f\in A\), \(f\ne 0\), such that
107	  %  the localisation \(M_f\) is a free module over \(A_f\)."
108	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, ``Lemma on Generic Flatness''.}
109	  Let \(A\) be a noetherian domain, \(B\) a finite-type \(A\)-algebra, and \(M\) a finite
110	  \(B\)-module. Then there exists \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), such that the localisation
111	  \(M_f\) is a free \(A_f\)-module.
112	\end{theorem}
113	
114	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Over the quotient field \(K\) of \(A\), the \(K\)-algebra
115	% \(B_K = K\otimes _AB\) is of finite type, and \(M_K = K\otimes_AM\) is a
116	% finite module over \(B_K\). Let \(n\) be the dimension of the support
117	% of \(M_K\) over \(\spec B_K\). We argue by induction on \(n\), starting
118	% with \(n=-1\) which is the case when \(M_K =0\). [...]
119	% Now let \(n\ge 0\), and let the lemma be proved for smaller values.
120	% As \(B\) is noetherian and \(M\) is assumed to be a finite \(B\)-module,
121	% there exists a finite filtration
122	% \(0=M_0 \subset \ldots  \subset M_r =M\)
123	% where each \(M_i\) is a \(B\)-submodule of \(M\) such that for each \(i\ge 1\)
124	% the quotient module \(M_i/M_{i-1}\) is isomorphic to \(B/\pp_i\) for
125	% some prime ideal \(\pp_i\) in \(B\). [...] By Noether normalisation lemma,
126	% there exist elements \(b_1,\ldots,b_n \in B\), such that \(K\otimes_AB\) is
127	% finite over its subalgebra \(K[b_1,\ldots, b_n]\) [...]"
128	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1719-L1772; full
129	% proof omitted for length, structural steps restated below in project notation.)
130	\begin{proof}
131	  
132	  Following [Nitsure], \S4: pass to the fraction field \(K = \mathrm{Frac}(A)\) and
133	  set \(B_K = K \otimes_A B\), \(M_K = K \otimes_A M\), \(n := \dim \mathrm{supp}_{\Spec B_K} M_K\).
134	  Argue by induction on \(n\), with base case \(n = -1\) (i.e.\ \(M_K = 0\)): each
135	  generator of \(M\) is annihilated by some non-zero element of \(A\); taking the
136	  product of finitely many annihilators yields \(f \in A\) with \(f M = 0\), hence
137	  \(M_f = 0\) is trivially free.  For \(n \ge 0\), a prime filtration of \(M\) as a
138	  finite \(B\)-module
139	  \[
140	    0 = M_0 \subset M_1 \subset \cdots \subset M_r = M
141	  \]
142	  with successive quotients \(M_i / M_{i-1} \cong B / \mathfrak{p}_i\) reduces the
143	  problem (via the splicing fact: \(M_{f'f''}\) is \(A_{f'f''}\)-free when \(M'_{f'}\)
144	  and \(M''_{f''}\) are free) to the case \(M = B/\mathfrak{p}\) for a prime
145	  \(\mathfrak{p} \subset B\), then to the case \(B\) a domain and \(M = B\).
146	  Noether normalisation applied to \(B_K / K\) yields algebraically independent
147	  \(b_1, \dots, b_n \in B\) with \(B_K\) finite over \(K[b_1, \dots, b_n]\); choosing
148	  \(g \ne 0\) to clear denominators of integrality equations, \(B_g\) is finite over
149	  \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\). Let \(m\) be the generic rank: the short exact sequence
150	  \[
151	    0 \to A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]^{\oplus m} \to B_g \to T \to 0
152	  \]
153	  has \(T\) a finite torsion module over \(A_g[b_1, \dots, b_n]\), so $\dim
154	  \mathrm{supp}_{B_K} (K \otimes_{A_g} T) < n$.  The inductive hypothesis
155	  yields \(h \ne 0\) in \(A\) with \(T_h\) free over \(A_{gh}\); set \(f := gh\).
156	\end{proof}
157	
158	\begin{theorem}
159	
160	\leanok
161	[Generic flatness, geometric form]
162	  \label{thm:generic_flatness}
163	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness}
164	  \uses{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}
165	
166	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''
167	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1781-L1787).
168	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
169	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian and integral scheme.
170	  %  Let \(p: X\to S\) be a finite type morphism, and let \(\F\) be a
171	  %  coherent sheaf of \({\mathcal O}_X\)-modules. Then there exists a non-empty
172	  %  open subscheme \(U\subset S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\)
173	  %  to \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is flat over \({\mathcal O}_U\)."
174	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``generic flatness''.}
175	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian integral scheme, \(p : X \to S\) a finite-type morphism,
176	  and \(\F\) a coherent \(\OO_X\)-module. Then there exists a non-empty open
177	  subscheme \(U \subseteq S\) such that the restriction of \(\F\) to
178	  \(X_U = p^{-1}(U)\) is \(\OO_U\)-flat.
179	\end{theorem}
180	
181	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The above theorem has the following consequence, which
182	% follows by restricting attention to a non-empty affine open subscheme of \(S\)."
183	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1776-L1777. The
184	% Nitsure proof is the single sentence quoted: deduce the geometric form from
185	% the algebraic generic flatness statement (\cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic})
186	% by restricting to a non-empty
187	% affine open of \(S\), where the situation reduces to the algebraic statement.)
188	\begin{proof}
189	  
190	  Restrict to a non-empty affine open \(\Spec A \subseteq S\) where \(A\) is a
191	  noetherian domain. The morphism \(p\) is of finite type, so locally on \(X\)
192	  above this affine \(\F\) is the sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\) for \(M\) a finite module
193	  over a finite-type \(A\)-algebra \(B\). \cref{thm:generic_flatness_algebraic}
194	  produces \(f \in A\), \(f \ne 0\), with \(M_f\) free over \(A_f\) on each such patch;
195	  shrinking to the basic open \(D(f) \subseteq \Spec A\) globalises by patching
196	  finitely many such opens (using noetherianity of \(X\) above the affine).
197	\end{proof}

199	\section{Existence of the flattening stratification}
200	\label{sec:flatstrat_exists}
201	
202	\begin{theorem}
203	
204	\leanok
205	[Existence of the flattening stratification]
206	  \label{thm:flattening_stratification_exists}
207	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatteningStratification}
208	  \uses{def:coherent_sheaf_flat, lem:flat_locus_open, lem:nonflat_locus_proper,
209	    lem:noetherian_induction_strata, thm:generic_flatness}
210	
211	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``flattening stratification''
212	  % (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1811-L1841).
213	  % Parallel pointer: [Stacks Project], tag 052H (verbatim text not yet
214	  % retrieved into a local references/ file; see ``Notes for Plan Agent'').
215	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
216	  % "Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, and let
217	  %  \(\F\) be a coherent sheaf on the projective space \(\PP^n_S\)
218	  %  over \(S\). Then the set \(I\) of Hilbert polynomials of
219	  %  restrictions of \(\F\) to fibers of \(\PP^n_S\to S\)
220	  %  is a finite set. Moreover, for each \(f\in I\) there exist
221	  %  a locally closed subscheme \(S_f\) of \(S\),
222	  %  such that the following conditions are satisfied.
223	  %
224	  %  \textbf{(i) Point-set: } The underlying set \(|S_f|\) of \(S_f\) consists of
225	  %  all points \(s\in S\) where the Hilbert polynomial of the
226	  %  restriction of \(\F\) to \(\PP^n_s\) is \(f\). In particular,
227	  %  the subsets \(|S_f|\subset |S|\) are disjoint,
228	  %  and their set-theoretic union is \(|S|\).
229	  %
230	  %  \textbf{(ii) Universal property: }
231	  %  Let \(S'=\coprod S_f\) be the coproduct of the \(S_f\), and let
232	  %  \(i:S'\to S\) be the morphism induced by the inclusions
233	  %  \(S_f\hra S\). Then the sheaf \(i^*(\F)\) on \(\PP^n_{S'}\)
234	  %  is flat over \(S'\). Moreover, \(i:S'\to S\) has the universal property that
235	  %  for any morphism \(\varphi :T\to S\) the pullback \(\varphi^*(\F)\) on
236	  %  \(\PP^n_T\) is flat over \(T\) if and only if \(\varphi\) factors through
237	  %  \(i:S'\to S\).  The subscheme \(S_f\) is
238	  %  uniquely determined by the polynomial \(f\).
239	  %
240	  %  \textbf{(iii) Closure of strata: }
241	  %  Let the set \(I\) of Hilbert polynomials be given a total
242	  %  ordering, defined by putting \(f<g\) whenever \(f(n) < g(n)\) for all
243	  %  \(n \gg  0\). Then the closure in \(S\) of the subset \(|S_f|\)
244	  %  is contained in the union of all \(|S_g|\) where \(f\le g\)."
245	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``flattening stratification'';
246	  cf.\ [Stacks Project], tag 052H.}
247	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and \(\F\) a coherent sheaf on the relative
248	  projective space \(\PP^n_S\) over \(S\).  Then:
249	  \begin{enumerate}[label=(\roman*)]
250	    \item The set \(I\) of Hilbert polynomials
251	      \(P_s(m) = \chi(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) that arise as \(s\) ranges over \(S\) is
252	      finite.
253	    \item For each \(f \in I\) there exists a locally-closed subscheme
254	      \(S_f \hookrightarrow S\) whose underlying set \(|S_f|\) consists of exactly
255	      those \(s \in S\) with \(P_s = f\).  The collection \(\{|S_f|\}_{f \in I}\) is
256	      pairwise disjoint with set-theoretic union \(|S|\).  Forming the coproduct
257	      \(S' := \coprod_{f \in I} S_f\) and the morphism \(i : S' \to S\) assembled
258	      from the inclusions \(S_f \hookrightarrow S\), the pullback \(i^* \F\) on
259	      \(\PP^n_{S'}\) is flat over \(S'\), and \(i : S' \to S\) is universal with this
260	      property: for any morphism \(\varphi : T \to S\) such that \(\varphi^* \F\) on
261	      \(\PP^n_T\) is flat over \(T\), \(\varphi\) factors uniquely through \(i\).
262	      Each \(S_f\) is uniquely determined by \(f\).
263	    \item Order \(I\) by \(f < g \iff f(n) < g(n)\) for all \(n \gg 0\). Then the
264	      closure of \(|S_f|\) in \(|S|\) is contained in \(\bigcup_{g \ge f} |S_g|\).
265	  \end{enumerate}
266	\end{theorem}
267	
268	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "It is enough to prove the theorem for open subschemes of
269	% $S$ which cover $S$, as the resulting strata will then glue together by their
270	% universal property. [...] \textbf{General case: } We now allow the integer
271	% $n$ to be arbitrary. The idea of the proof is as follows: We show the
272	% existence of a stratification of $S$ which is a `g.c.d.' of the flattening
273	% stratifications for direct images $\pi_*\F(i)$ for all $i \ge N$ for some
274	% integer $N$ (where the flattening stratifications for $\pi_*\F(i)$ exist by
275	% case $n=0$ which we have treated above). This is the desired flattening
276	% stratification of $\F$ over $S$, as follows from Lemma \ref{graded module is flat}."
277	% (Source: nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1844-L1900; the
278	% full body L1844-L2090 is distributed across the sub-lemmas below, each of
279	% which carries its own verbatim % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF.)
280	\begin{proof}
281	  \uses{def:coherent_sheaf_flat, lem:flat_locus_open, lem:nonflat_locus_proper,
282	    lem:noetherian_induction_strata, thm:generic_flatness}
283	  Following [Nitsure], \S4, the construction reduces to the special case
284	  \(n = 0\), assembled over the relative twists \(\pi_* \F(N+i)\).  As the
285	  argument is local on \(S\) (the strata glue by their universal property, part
286	  (ii)), and as the three conclusions are proved through the sub-lemmas below,
287	  we assemble them in the order (i), (ii), (iii).
288	
289	  \emph{Part (i): finiteness of the Hilbert-polynomial set.}
290	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:nonflat_locus_proper}, Noetherian induction on \(S\) (whose
291	  inductive step invokes generic flatness, Theorem~\ref{thm:generic_flatness},
292	  on the integral locally-closed strata cut out by the irreducible-component
293	  decomposition of \(S\)) produces finitely many reduced, pairwise disjoint,
294	  locally-closed subschemes \(V_i \hookrightarrow S\) set-theoretically covering
295	  \(|S|\) on each of which \(\F|_{\PP^n_{V_i}}\) is flat over \(\OO_{V_i}\) in
296	  the sense of Definition~\ref{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}.  On a flat family the
297	  Hilbert polynomial \(P_s(m) = \chi(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) is locally constant, so
298	  it takes finitely many values on each quasi-compact \(V_i\); the finite union
299	  over the finitely many \(V_i\) shows that the set \(I\) of Hilbert polynomials
300	  occurring on \(S\) is finite.
301	
302	  \emph{Part (ii): existence of the strata and their universal property.}
303	  Working over the flat pieces \(V_i\) of part (i), semicontinuity
304	  (cohomology-and-base-change) yields a uniform integer \(N\) such that, for all
305	  \(s \in S\) and \(m \ge N\), the higher cohomologies \(H^r(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\)
306	  vanish for \(r \ge 1\) and the base-change homomorphism
307	  \((\pi_* \F(m))|_s \to H^0(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) is an isomorphism.  With this
308	  \(N\) fixed, Lemma~\ref{lem:noetherian_induction_strata} applies the \(n = 0\)
309	  stratification mechanism (Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_locus_open}) successively to the
310	  coherent sheaves \(E_i = \pi_* \F(N+i)\) on \(S\) for \(i = 0, \dots, n\),
311	  refining stratum by stratum.  After \(n+1\) steps this produces a finite
312	  family of locally-closed subschemes \(W_{e_0, \dots, e_n} \subseteq S\),
313	  universal for the property that each \(E_i\) pulls back to a locally-free sheaf
314	  of constant rank \(e_i\); re-indexing the tuple \((e_0, \dots, e_n)\) by the
315	  unique numerical polynomial \(f\) of degree \(\le n\) with \(f(N+i) = e_i\)
316	  gives the family \(\{W_f\}_{f \in I}\).  At any \(s \in W_f\) the base-change
317	  isomorphism and the cohomology vanishing force \(P_s = f\) (since \(P_s\) has
318	  degree \(\le n\) and is determined by its values at \(N, \dots, N+n\)), so
319	  \(|W_f| = \{s : P_s = f\}\); these subsets are disjoint with union \(|S|\).
320	  The required locally-closed subscheme \(S_f \subseteq W_f\) is the closed
321	  subscheme cut out by the further local-freeness conditions
322	  ``\(\pi_* \F(N+i)|_{W_f}\) is locally free of rank \(f(N+i)\) for all
323	  \(i \ge 0\)'' (a chain of coherent ideal sheaves that stabilises by
324	  Noetherianity), and \(\F|_{\PP^n_{S_f}}\) is flat over \(S_f\)
325	  (Definition~\ref{def:coherent_sheaf_flat}) because the graded module
326	  \(\bigoplus_{i \gg 0} \pi_* \F(i)|_{S_f}\) is locally free.  Forming
327	  \(S' = \coprod_{f \in I} S_f\) and the assembled morphism \(i : S' \to S\),
328	  the pullback \(i^* \F\) is \(S'\)-flat; and any \(\varphi : T \to S\) with
329	  \(\varphi^* \F\) flat over \(T\) has locally constant Hilbert polynomial, hence
330	  decomposes \(T = \coprod_f T_f\) into the open-closed loci where the polynomial
331	  is \(f\), each factoring uniquely through the corresponding \(S_f\) by the
332	  local-freeness universal property of Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_locus_open}.  This
333	  gives the unique factorisation through \(i\), and shows each \(S_f\) is
334	  uniquely determined by \(f\).
335	
336	  \emph{Part (iii): closure of strata.}
337	  Order \(I\) by \(f < g \iff f(n) < g(n)\) for all \(n \gg 0\).  Since each
338	  \(S_f\) is a stratum of the \(n = 0\) flattening stratification applied to a
339	  single direct image \(\pi_* \F(p)\) for \(p \gg 0\) separating all polynomials
340	  in \(I\), the closure-of-strata assertion reduces to the upper-semicontinuity
341	  statement already established in the \(n = 0\) case
342	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_locus_open}): the closure of \(|S_f|\) in \(|S|\) is
343	  contained in \(\bigcup_{g \ge f} |S_g|\).
344	\end{proof}
345	
346	The proof is long; we split it into sub-lemmas below.  The first sub-lemma is
347	the special case \(n = 0\) (when \(\PP^n_S = S\) and \(\F\) is just a coherent sheaf
348	on \(S\)), which already contains the flat-locus-as-locally-closed-subscheme
349	mechanism. The remaining inputs are (A) the Noetherian-induction reduction to
350	the flat case via generic flatness; (B) uniform vanishing of higher cohomology
351	of \(\F(m)\) for \(m \gg 0\) on \(\F_{V_i}\) for the flat pieces; and (C) base-change
352	isomorphisms for the direct images \(\pi_* \F(m)\), which allow comparing
353	strata for different twists.
354	
355	\begin{lemma}
356	[Flat locus on \(S\) for a coherent sheaf is a locally-closed stratification]
357	  \label{lem:flat_locus_open}
358	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.flatLocusOpen}
359	
360	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``flattening stratification'', Special case
361	  % \(n=0\) (read from references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
362	  % L1849-L1885).
363	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
364	  % "\textbf{Special case: } Let \(n=0\), so that \(\PP^n_S=S\).
365	  %  For any \(s\in S\), the \textbf{fiber} \(\F|_s\) of \(\F\) over \(s\)
366	  %  will mean the pull-back of \(\F\) to the subscheme
367	  %  \(\spec \kappa(s)\) [...]. The Hilbert polynomial of the
368	  %  restriction of \(\F\) to the fiber over \(s\) is the degree \(0\)
369	  %  polynomial \(e \in \Q[\lambda]\), where \(e = \dim_{\kappa(s)}\F|_s\).
370	  %
371	  %  By Nakayama lemma, any basis of \(\F|_s\) prolongs to a neighbourhood \(U\) of \(s\)
372	  %  to give a set of generators for \(F|_U\). Repeating this argument,
373	  %  we see that there exists a smaller neighbourhood \(V\) of \(s\) in which
374	  %  there is a right-exact sequence
375	  %  ${\mathcal O}_V^{\oplus m} \stackrel{\psi}{\to} {\mathcal O}_V^{\oplus e}
376	  %  \stackrel{\phi}{\to} \F \to 0$.
377	  %  Let \(I_e\subset {\mathcal O}_V\) be the ideal sheaf
378	  %  formed by the entries of the \(e\times m\) matrix
379	  %  \((\psi_{i,j})\) [...].
380	  %  Let \(V_e\) be the closed subscheme of \(V\) defined by \(I_e\).
381	  %  For any morphism of schemes
382	  %  \(f: T\to V\), the pull-back sequence [...]
383	  %  is exact, by right-exactness of tensor products.
384	  %  Hence the pull-back \(f^*\F\) is a locally free \({\mathcal O}_T\)-module of
385	  %  rank \(e\) if and only if \(f^*\psi =0\), that is,
386	  %  \(f\) factors via the subscheme \(V_e\hra V\) defined by the vanishing of all
387	  %  entries \(\psi_{i,j}\).
388	  %  Thus we have proved assertions \textbf{(i)} and \textbf{(ii)} of the theorem.
389	  %
390	  %  As the rank of the matrix \((\psi_{i,j})\) is lower semi-continuous,
391	  %  it follows that the function \(e\) is upper semi-continuous, which
392	  %  proves the assertion \textbf{(iii)} of the theorem, completing its proof
393	  %  when \(n=0\)."
394	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (special case \(n=0\) of the main theorem).}
395	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(\F\) be a coherent \(\OO_S\)-module.
396	  For each integer \(e \ge 0\), there exists a locally-closed subscheme
397	  \(S_e \hookrightarrow S\) whose underlying set is
398	  \(\{s \in S : \dim_{\kappa(s)} \F|_s = e\}\). The family \(\{S_e\}_e\) is a
399	  finite, mutually disjoint, set-theoretically covering stratification of \(S\),
400	  and a morphism \(f : T \to S\) pulls back \(\F\) to a locally-free
401	  \(\OO_T\)-module of rank \(e\) if and only if \(f\) factors through \(S_e\).  The
402	  function \(s \mapsto \dim_{\kappa(s)} \F|_s\) is upper-semicontinuous on \(S\).
403	\end{lemma}
404	
405	\begin{proof}
406	  
407	  By [Nitsure], \S4 (verbatim above): for \(s \in S\), Nakayama produces a
408	  neighbourhood \(V\) of \(s\) and a right-exact local presentation
409	  \(\OO_V^{\oplus m} \xrightarrow{\psi} \OO_V^{\oplus e} \to \F|_V \to 0\) where
410	  \(e = \dim_{\kappa(s)} \F|_s\).  The closed subscheme \(V_e \hookrightarrow V\)
411	  defined by the ideal sheaf generated by the matrix entries of \(\psi\)
412	  represents the locus where \(\F\) becomes locally free of rank \(e\): by
413	  right-exactness of tensor product, a base change \(f : T \to V\) has
414	  \(f^* \F\) locally free of rank \(e\) iff \(f^* \psi = 0\), iff \(f\) factors
415	  through \(V_e\).  Glueing the \(V_e\) over the cover of \(S\) by such
416	  neighbourhoods (and intersecting with \(V \setminus \bigcup_{e' > e} V_{e'}\)
417	  to get the strictly-equal-to-\(e\) stratum) produces the locally-closed
418	  \(S_e\). Upper-semicontinuity of \(e\) follows because the rank of \(\psi\) is
419	  lower-semicontinuous.
420	\end{proof}
421	
422	\begin{lemma}
423	[Noetherian induction reduction to the flat case]
424	  \label{lem:nonflat_locus_proper}
425	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.nonflatLocusProper}
426	  \uses{thm:generic_flatness}
427	
428	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``flattening stratification'', General case
429	  % opening paragraphs (read from
430	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1888-L1929).
431	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
432	  % "\textbf{General case: } We now allow the integer \(n\) to be arbitrary.
433	  %  The idea of the proof is as follows:
434	  %  We show the existence of a stratification
435	  %  of \(S\) which is a `g.c.d.' of the flattening
436	  %  stratifications for direct images \(\pi_*\F(i)\) for all \(i \ge N\) for some
437	  %  integer \(N\) [...].
438	  %
439	  %  As \(S\) is noetherian, it is a finite union of irreducible components,
440	  %  and these are closed in \(S\).
441	  %  Let \(Y\) be an irreducible component of \(S\), and let \(U\) be the
442	  %  non-empty open subset of \(Y\) which consists of all points which do not
443	  %  lie on any other irreducible component of \(S\).
444	  %  Let \(U\) be given the reduced subscheme
445	  %  structure. Note that this makes \(U\) an integral scheme, which is a
446	  %  locally closed subscheme of \(S\).
447	  %  By Theorem \ref{generic flatness} on generic flatness,
448	  %  \(U\) has a non-empty open subscheme \(V\) such that
449	  %  the restriction of \(\F\) to \(\PP^n_V\) is flat over \({\mathcal O}_V\).
450	  %  Now repeating the argument with \(S\) replaced by its reduced
451	  %  closed subscheme \(S-V\),
452	  %  it follows by noetherian induction on \(S\) that there exist
453	  %  finitely many reduced, locally closed, mutually disjoint subschemes
454	  %  \(V_i\) of \(S\) such that set-theoretically
455	  %  \(|S|\) is the union of the \(|V_i|\) and
456	  %  the restriction of \(\F\) to \(\PP^n_{V_i}\) is flat over \({\mathcal O}_{V_i}\).
457	  %  As each \(V_i\) is a noetherian scheme, and as the Hilbert polynomials
458	  %  are locally constant for a flat family of sheaves, it follows
459	  %  that only finitely many polynomials occur in \(V_i\)
460	  %  in the family of Hilbert polynomials \(P_s(m) = \chi(\PP^n_s,\F_s(m))\)
461	  %  as \(s\) varies over points of \(V_i\). This allows us to conclude
462	  %  the following:
463	  %
464	  %  \textbf{(A) } Only finitely many distinct Hilbert polynomials
465	  %  \(P_s(m) = \chi(\PP^n_s,\F_s(m))\) occur, as \(s\)
466	  %  varies over all of \(S\)."
467	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (general case, reduction by Noetherian induction).}
468	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(\pi : \PP^n_S \to S\) the relative projective
469	  space, and \(\F\) a coherent sheaf on \(\PP^n_S\).  Then there exist finitely many
470	  reduced, locally-closed, pairwise disjoint subschemes \(V_i \hookrightarrow S\)
471	  whose set-theoretic union is \(|S|\) and such that \(\F|_{\PP^n_{V_i}}\) is
472	  flat over \(\OO_{V_i}\) for each \(i\). Consequently, only finitely many
473	  Hilbert polynomials \(P_s(m) = \chi(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) occur as \(s\) varies
474	  over \(S\).
475	\end{lemma}
476	
477	\begin{proof}
478	  
479	  By [Nitsure], \S4: \(S\) being noetherian, decompose \(|S| = \bigcup Y_j\) into
480	  finitely many irreducible components, each closed.  For an irreducible
481	  component \(Y\), let \(U \subseteq Y\) be the open subset not lying on any other
482	  component, given the reduced subscheme structure (an integral, locally-closed
483	  subscheme of \(S\)).  By \cref{thm:generic_flatness} applied to
484	  \(\F|_{\PP^n_U}\), there is a non-empty open \(V \subseteq U\) over which \(\F\) is
485	  flat.  Replace \(S\) by the reduced closed complement \(S \setminus V\) and
486	  repeat. Noetherianity of \(S\) terminates the induction in finitely many
487	  steps, producing the \(V_i\).  On each \(V_i\) the Hilbert polynomial is locally
488	  constant (flat families have locally constant Hilbert polynomial), hence
489	  takes finitely many values on the noetherian (hence quasi-compact) scheme
490	  \(V_i\); the finite union of finite sets is finite.
491	\end{proof}

493	\begin{lemma}
494	[Assembly of the strata by Noetherian induction]
495	  \label{lem:noetherian_induction_strata}
496	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.TODO.noetherianInductionStrata}
497	  \uses{lem:flat_locus_open, lem:nonflat_locus_proper, thm:flat_base_change_cohomology}
498	
499	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem ``flattening stratification'', Assembly via
500	  % direct images \(E_i = \pi_*\F(N+i)\) (read from
501	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L1992-L2090).
502	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
503	  % "Let \(\pi : \PP^n_S \to S\) denote the projection. Consider the
504	  %  coherent sheaves \(E_0, \ldots, E_n\) on \(S\), defined by
505	  %  \(E_i = \pi_*\F(N+i)\mbox{ for } i=0,\ldots,n.\)
506	  %  By applying the special case of of the theorem (where the relative dimension
507	  %  \(n\) of \(\PP^n_S\) is \(0\)) to the sheaf \(E_0\) on \(\PP^0_S=S\), we get
508	  %  a stratification \((W_{e_0})\) of \(S\)
509	  %  indexed by integers \(e_0\), such that for any morphism
510	  %  \(f: T\to S\) the pull-back \(f^*E_0\) is a locally free \({\mathcal O}_T\)-module
511	  %  of rank \(e_0\) if and only if \(f\) factors via \(W_{e_0} \hra S\).
512	  %  Next, for each stratum \(W_{e_0}\), we take the
513	  %  flattening stratification \((W_{e_0,e_1})\) for \(E_1|_{W_{e_0}}\), and so on.
514	  %  Thus in \(n+1\) steps, we obtain finitely many
515	  %  locally closed subschemes
516	  %  \(W_{e_0,\ldots,e_n} \subset S\)
517	  %  such that for any morphism \(f:T\to S\) the pull-back
518	  %  \(f^*E_i\) for \(i=0,\ldots,n\) is a locally free \({\mathcal O}_T\)-modules of
519	  %  of constant rank \(e_i\) if and only if \(f\) factors
520	  %  via \(W_{e_0,\ldots,e_n} \hra S\). [...]
521	  %  This shows that at any point \(s\in W_f\), the Hilbert polynomial
522	  %  \(P_s(m)\) equals \(f\). The desired locally closed subscheme \(S_f \subset S\),
523	  %  whose existence is asserted by the theorem,
524	  %  will turn out to be a certain closed subscheme \(S_f \subset W_f\) whose
525	  %  underlying subset is all of \(|W_f|\). [...]
526	  %  As \(S_f\) is the flattening stratification for \(\pi_*\F(p)\),
527	  %  the property (iii) of the theorem follows from the corresponding property in
528	  %  the case \(n=0\), applied to the sheaf \(\pi_*\F(p)\) on \(S\)."
529	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S4 (assembly step combining the \(n=0\) special case
530	  applied to direct images \(\pi_*\F(N+i)\)).}
531	  Let \(S\), \(\pi : \PP^n_S \to S\), \(\F\) be as in
532	  \cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists}.  There exists an integer
533	  \(N \ge 0\) such that for every \(s \in S\) the higher cohomologies
534	  \(H^r(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) vanish for all \(r \ge 1\), \(m \ge N\), and the base-change
535	  homomorphism \((\pi_* \F(m))|_s \to H^0(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) is an isomorphism
536	  for \(m \ge N\), \(s \in S\).  Iterating the \(n=0\) stratification
537	  (\cref{lem:flat_locus_open}) on the coherent sheaves \(E_i := \pi_* \F(N+i)\)
538	  for \(i = 0, \dots, n\) produces a finite collection of locally-closed
539	  subschemes \(W_{e_0, \dots, e_n} \subseteq S\) indexed by tuples
540	  \((e_0, \dots, e_n) \in \Z^{n+1}\). Re-indexing each tuple by the unique
541	  numerical polynomial \(f \in \Q[\lambda]\) of degree \(\le n\) with
542	  \(f(N+i) = e_i\) gives a finite stratification \(\{W_f\}\) of \(S\), and the
543	  locally-closed subschemes \(S_f \subseteq W_f\) of
544	  \cref{thm:flattening_stratification_exists} are obtained by further
545	  intersecting with the closed-subscheme conditions that
546	  \(\pi_* \F(N+i)|_{W_f}\) be locally free of rank \(f(N+i)\) for all \(i \ge 0\)
547	  (terminating by Noetherianity).
548	\end{lemma}
549	
550	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: full proof is the long body of [Nitsure], \S4, Theorem
551	% ``flattening stratification'', spanning L1844-L2090 (~250 lines: special
552	% case, Noetherian induction, statements (A)/(B)/(C), then the assembly via
553	% direct images and the closed-subscheme construction inside each \(W_f\)).
554	% Verbatim retrieved per-sub-lemma above; the structural restatement below in
555	% project notation is the canonical Nitsure-style restated proof.
556	\begin{proof}
557	  
558	  By \cref{lem:nonflat_locus_proper}, the family of Hilbert polynomials
559	  \(\{P_s\}\) on \(S\) is finite, and there is a finite stratification
560	  \(\{V_i\}\) of \(S\) on each of which \(\F\) is flat with locally-constant
561	  Hilbert polynomial.  Apply semi-continuity (cohomology and base change,
562	  [Hartshorne], III.12 / [Stacks Project], tag 02KH for the \(H^0\)-version) on
563	  the noetherian \(V_i\) to obtain a uniform integer \(N_1\) with
564	  \(R^r \pi_* \F(m) = 0\) for \(r \ge 1\), \(m \ge N_1\), and \(H^r(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m)) = 0\)
565	  for all \(s \in S\), \(r \ge 1\), \(m \ge N_1\) (statement (B) of Nitsure's proof).
566	  Choose \(r_i \ge N_1\) on each \(V_i\) such that the base-change comparison
567	  \((\pi_* \F(m))|_{V_i} \to (\pi_i)_* \F_{V_i}(m)\) is an isomorphism for
568	  \(m \ge r_i\); composing with the cohomology-and-base-change isomorphism
569	  \(((\pi_i)_* \F_{V_i}(m))|_s \xrightarrow{\sim} H^0(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) for
570	  the flat family on \(V_i\) yields uniform \(N = \max r_i\) such that
571	  \((\pi_* \F(m))|_s \to H^0(\PP^n_s, \F_s(m))\) is an isomorphism for \(m \ge N\)
572	  and all \(s \in S\) (statement (C)).
573	
574	  Apply \cref{lem:flat_locus_open} sequentially to the coherent sheaves
575	  \(E_i = \pi_* \F(N+i)\) on \(S\) for \(i = 0, \dots, n\): \(E_0\) produces a
576	  stratification \(\{W_{e_0}\}\) of \(S\); on each \(W_{e_0}\) apply the lemma to
577	  \(E_1|_{W_{e_0}}\) to refine to \(\{W_{e_0, e_1}\}\), and continue. After \(n+1\)
578	  steps the \(W_{e_0, \dots, e_n}\) form a finite locally-closed stratification
579	  of \(S\) universal for the property ``each \(E_i\) pulls back to a locally-free
580	  \(\OO_T\)-module of constant rank \(e_i\)''.  Re-index \((e_0, \dots, e_n)\) by the
581	  unique numerical polynomial \(f\) of degree \(\le n\) with \(f(N+i) = e_i\) to get
582	  the family \(\{W_f\}\) indexed by polynomials.  At any \(s \in W_f\), statement
583	  (C) and vanishing (B) force \(P_s = f\) (since \(P_s\) has degree \(\le n\) and is
584	  determined by its values at \(N, N+1, \dots, N+n\)).
585	
586	  The Hilbert-polynomial-coincidence locus \(|W_f|\) may have non-flat scheme
587	  structure; refine to \(S_f \subseteq W_f\) by intersecting with the
588	  closed-subscheme conditions ``\(\pi_* \F(N+i)|_{W_f}\) is locally-free of rank
589	  \(f(N+i)\)'' for all \(i \ge 0\).  These conditions are cut out by an increasing
590	  chain of coherent ideal sheaves \(I_0 \subseteq I_0 + I_1 \subseteq \cdots\) on
591	  \(W_f\); Noetherianity terminates the chain in finitely many steps, defining a
592	  coherent ideal sheaf \(I\) and a closed subscheme \(S_f \subseteq W_f\) with
593	  \(|S_f| = |W_f|\) on which \(\pi_* \F(N+i)\) is locally-free of rank \(f(N+i)\)
594	  for all \(i \ge 0\). The base-change-without-flatness lemma (cf.\ [Nitsure],
595	  \S3) yields some \(N' \ge N\) such that for \(i \ge N'\),
596	  \((\pi_* \F(i))|_{S_f} \to (\pi_{S_f})_* \F_{S_f}(i)\) is an isomorphism, hence
597	  the graded \(A\)-module \(\bigoplus_{i \ge N'} (\pi_{S_f})_* \F_{S_f}(i)\) is
598	  locally-free on \(S_f\), hence \(\F_{S_f}\) is flat over \(S_f\). The
599	  closure-of-strata property (iii) reduces to the corresponding property in
600	  the \(n=0\) case for \(\pi_* \F(p)\) on \(S\) at any \(p \ge N\) separating all
601	  Hilbert polynomials.
602	\end{proof}
